claim: WHO Global Status Report 2023 estimates 1.3 million annual road traffic deaths, over 90 percent in LMICs, highest death rates in Africa and Southeast Asia, and post-crash care as one of five road-safety pillars.

source.title: Global Status Report on Road Safety 2023

method: Reviewed report chapters on mortality, regional trends, risk factors, and post-crash care.
limitations: Death counts depend on national reporting systems of variable completeness.
Crash-location data is not systematically collected in most LMICs.

claim: A 2022 systematic review found that fewer than 30 percent of injured persons in LMIC cities reach a trauma facility within the golden hour, with the largest delays in informal settlements and peri-urban areas.

source.title: Access to trauma care in low- and middle-income countries: a systematic review

method: Reviewed systematic search, access-time measurement methods, and urban-rural comparisons.
limitations: Access-time studies are concentrated in a small number of LMIC cities.
